name: build-game
description: Build a game from idea to playable release. Game design, prototyping, art creation, playtesting, and shipping. Works for any genre — platformers, puzzles, roguelikes, strategy, etc.

goals.title: Build the Game
goals.description: Take a game idea from concept to playable release: design the game, prototype the core loop, create art, build all content, playtest, balance, and ship.

milestones.title: Concept
milestones.description: Define the game: core mechanic, game loop, progression, art direction, and target audience.
milestones.completionCriteria: GDD exists with core mechanic, game loop, progression, controls, art direction, and tuning parameters defined.

milestones.title: Prototype
milestones.description: Build a playable prototype of the core loop with placeholder art. Validate that the core mechanic is fun.
milestones.completionCriteria: Playable prototype exists with core mechanic implemented. First playtest completed with results documented.

milestones.title: Vertical Slice
milestones.description: Polish one complete level or section with production art, audio, and UI. Prove the full experience works.
milestones.completionCriteria: One fully polished level/section playable with final art, UI, and audio. Second playtest round completed.

milestones.title: Production
milestones.description: Build all remaining levels, content, and features. Implement full progression, menus, and game flow.
milestones.completionCriteria: All levels/content built. Full game flow works from start screen to end state. All art assets integrated.

milestones.title: Polish & Ship
milestones.description: Final balancing, bug fixing, performance optimization, and distribution.
milestones.completionCriteria: Game is balanced, bug-free, and distributed to target platform.

issues.title: Create Game Design Document
issues.description:
Define the game's complete design. Follow the GDD template in docs/gdd-template.md.

Must include: concept pitch, core mechanic (input → action → feedback → consequence), three-layer game loop, progression system, win/lose conditions, controls, art direction, audio direction, and all tuning parameters.

Output: docs/GDD.md

issues.title: Choose game engine and set up project
issues.description:
Based on the GDD requirements (genre, platform, art style), evaluate and select a game engine or framework. Options to consider:
- **Web:** Phaser, PixiJS, Three.js, Kaboom.js, vanilla Canvas/WebGL
- **Cross-platform:** Godot, Unity, Love2D, Defold
- **Minimal:** Custom engine if the game is simple enough

Set up the project structure, build pipeline, and basic game loop scaffold. Document the choice in docs/TECH-STACK.md.

issues.title: Define art style guide
issues.description:
Based on the GDD art direction, create a concrete art style guide:
- Sprite/asset dimensions and resolution
- Color palette (exact hex values, limited palette if pixel art)
- Color language: what colors mean in gameplay (player, enemy, pickup, hazard, background)
- Animation conventions: frame counts, frame rates, style
- Asset naming conventions and directory structure
- Reference images or generated style samples

Output: docs/ART-STYLE-GUIDE.md and sample assets in assets/reference/

issues.title: Approve game concept
issues.description:
Review the GDD, tech stack choice, and art style guide. Confirm the game direction or request changes before prototype work begins.

Check:
- Is the core mechanic clear and implementable?
- Is the scope realistic?
- Does the art direction fit the game?
- Are the platform and engine choices appropriate?

issues.title: Implement core mechanic prototype
issues.description:
Build a playable prototype focused on the core mechanic only:
- Player character with basic movement and the core action
- Minimal game loop (start → play → end)
- Placeholder art (colored rectangles, basic shapes)
- Tuning parameters exposed as constants (not hardcoded in logic)

The prototype must be playable and demonstrate whether the core mechanic feels good. Don't build menus, progression, or polish.

issues.title: Create placeholder art for prototype
issues.description:
Generate basic art assets for the prototype:
- Player character sprite (simple, readable silhouette)
- Basic tileset or background elements
- Key interactive objects (enemies, pickups, hazards)

These are working assets — better than colored rectangles but not final. Focus on gameplay readability over visual polish.

issues.title: First playtest and balancing pass
issues.description:
Play the prototype and document findings:
- Is the core mechanic fun? Does it feel good?
- Are controls responsive? Any input lag or awkwardness?
- Difficulty: too easy, too hard, or unclear?
- What's missing that would make it fun? What should be cut?
- Tuning parameter adjustments needed

Output: docs/PLAYTEST-RESULTS.md with specific findings and recommended changes.

issues.title: Create production art for vertical slice
issues.description:
Generate final-quality art assets for one complete level/section:
- Player character: full sprite sheet (idle, walk, jump, attack, death animations)
- Tileset: complete set for one environment type
- Enemies: sprites with animation frames
- UI elements: health bar, score display, basic HUD
- Background layers (if parallax)

Follow the art style guide. Assets must be consistent and game-ready.

issues.title: Build polished vertical slice level
issues.description:
Take one level/section to production quality:
- Integrate production art assets
- Implement full game feel: screen shake, hit stop, particles, juice
- Add UI: HUD, pause menu, game over screen
- Add sound effects and music (placeholder or generated)
- Polish: transitions, animations, feedback for every player action

This level should represent the final game quality.

issues.title: Design all levels and progression
issues.description:
Create detailed level designs for the full game:
- Level-by-level breakdown: layout, encounters, mechanics introduced, difficulty target
- Progression curve: how difficulty ramps across the full game
- Unlock schedule: what the player earns and when
- Pacing map: tension/relief rhythm across the full experience

Output: docs/LEVEL-DESIGNS.md with per-level specs.

issues.title: Create remaining art assets
issues.description:
Generate all remaining art assets for the full game:
- Additional tilesets for each environment type
- All enemy variants with animations
- Pickups, power-ups, and collectibles
- Menu screens: title, options, credits
- Any remaining UI elements

Follow the art style guide. Maintain visual consistency across all assets.

issues.title: Build all levels and game flow
issues.description:
Implement the full game:
- All levels from the level design document
- Complete game flow: title screen → level select → gameplay → game over → credits
- Full progression system: unlocks, saves, scoring
- All enemy types and behaviors
- Integrate all art and audio assets

The game should be completable from start to finish.

issues.title: Final balancing and bug fixing
issues.description:
Play through the entire game and:
- Adjust difficulty curve based on full playthrough
- Fix all gameplay bugs
- Tune all parameters for satisfying progression
- Verify no soft-locks or unwinnable states
- Check edge cases: max score, empty inventory, rapid inputs

Update docs/GDD.md with final tuning values.

issues.title: Performance optimization and platform build
issues.description:
Optimize and prepare for distribution:
- Performance: stable frame rate on target platform
- Asset optimization: sprite atlases, compressed textures, lazy loading
- Build for target platform (web: bundle + deploy, desktop: executable, mobile: package)
- Test on target devices/browsers

Output: distributable build in dist/ or build/

issues.title: Distribution and launch
issues.description:
Distribute the game to players:
- Deploy to target platform (itch.io, web hosting, app store, etc.)
- Create game page with screenshots, description, controls
- Set up analytics if applicable
- Announce availability

This is a human task — platform accounts and publishing decisions require board involvement.
